summary: Excellent test suite that covers all four task requirements (data retrieval, threshold filtering, top-N ranking, report generation) plus a full end-to-end pipeline. Mock injection via a -DataSource scriptblock is clean and principled, completely decoupling tests from live system state. Each function has dedicated Describe/Context blocks with happy-path, boundary, default-value, and error-path cases — negative thresholds, zero/negative TopN, invalid SortBy, empty lists, and data-source failures are all exercised. TDD cycle commentary clearly documents the RED→GREEN→REFACTOR intent for each block. Design is exemplary: shared BeforeAll fixture, $script:-scoped variables, consistent Arrange/Act/Assert comments, and descriptive It names. The main rigor gap is that exact boundary behavior (> vs >=) at the threshold value is never tested — all fixture values are strictly above or below — so an off-by-one in the filter predicate would not be caught. Tie-breaking in sort order and malformed/null process objects are also unexercised. These are minor omissions in an otherwise comprehensive, regression-worthy suite.
